inference_steps: [7s to 10s; 60s to 62s] The main character is best grounded by the 7s-10s action sequence, because those frames repeatedly center one female tennis player in play, whereas the 60s-62s material is a later group scene.

[7s to 10s] In the verified main-character frames at 7s-10s, the player holds only her racket, with the tennis ball visible at 7s; no other held object is clearly shown. That means sunglasses, a towel, a wristband, and an energy bar are unsupported for the main character in the retrieved frames.

[60s; 7s to 10s] Although a water bottle is observed at 60s, the holder is not the same person as the main action-sequence player: the main player wears a white visor, white tank top, and dark skirt and has dark hair in a ponytail, while the water-bottle holder appears in a white t-shirt without a visor and has brown hair in a ponytail. So the water bottle should be rejected for the main character.

[7s to 10s; 60s] After rejecting water bottle and finding no support for wristband, sunglasses, towel, or energy bar in the main-character frames, none of the listed options is grounded as an object briefly held by the main character.

evidence.evidence_text: Frames at 7s, 8s, 9s, and 10s show the same female tennis player in action. She wears a white tank top, dark skirt, and white visor. In these frames she is shown holding a racket; at 7s a tennis ball is also visible in the air. No other object is clearly visible in her hands in these action frames.

The prompt refers to a single main female tennis player. The action sequence in frames 7s-10s clearly centers on one tennis player, while the later 60s-62s frames show a group of people rather than the same centered action subject.

At 60s, a girl in a white shirt is drinking from and holding a white water bottle in a later group scene. Visual comparison across the retrieved frames indicates she is not the same person as the main action-sequence player: the action-sequence player wears a white visor, white tank top, and dark skirt and has dark hair in a ponytail, while the water-bottle holder appears in a white t-shirt without a visor and has brown hair in a ponytail.
